name: Flower Fruit Mountain
description: Flower Fruit Mountain, or Huaguo Shan, is a legendary mountain in Chinese mythology, most famously known as the birthplace and home of Sun Wukong, the Monkey King. It is a paradisiacal island mountain, described as being incredibly beautiful and fertile, teeming with life and abundant resources. Covered in lush vegetation, waterfalls, and caves, it is a haven for monkeys and other animals. The mountain is also said to be connected to the underwater Dragon King's palace, granting access to mythical treasures and knowledge. Flower Fruit Mountain is not just a physical location but a symbol of freedom, independence, and the untamed spirit of nature. It represents a world outside the rigid structures of the celestial bureaucracy, a place where creativity and individuality can flourish. Its significance lies in its role as the foundation of Sun Wukong's journey and his eventual transformation into a powerful and respected figure in Chinese mythology. The mountain is often depicted as a utopia, a stark contrast to the hierarchical and often oppressive nature of the Jade Emperor's court.
subtitle: Monkey King's Birthplace and Home
features: Connection to the Dragon King's palace

keyMyths.title: Birth of Sun Wukong
keyMyths.description: Sun Wukong was born from a stone egg on the summit of Flower Fruit Mountain. This event marked the beginning of his legendary journey.

keyMyths.title: Discovery of the Waterfall Cave
keyMyths.description: Sun Wukong demonstrated bravery by jumping through a waterfall and discovering a cave behind it, earning him the title of Monkey King.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: The primary source for the mythology of Flower Fruit Mountain.
sources.text: The primary source for the mythology of Flower Fruit Mountain.

variants: The Mountain of Flowers and Fruit
